climate.description: March in Chiang Mai features dry, mild weather ideal for cycling, transitioning from cool mornings to warm afternoons.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67.

course_description.character: Rolling road gran fondo through Chiang Mai's lush mountains, rice terraces, and rural villages, featuring sustained climbs and fast descents in northern Thailand's scenic highlands.
course_description.signature_challenge: The 'Chiang Mai Wall' - a 3km climb at 9% average with sections hitting 14%, offering panoramic views of the Ping River Valley.
suffering_zones: Doi Inthanon Approach: Steep ramps leading to Thailand's highest peak, testing legs with 10-12% gradients amid misty forests.
Huai Kaeo Valley Climb: Long false flat haul turning into punchy rollers, where heat and humidity amplify fatigue.
Finish Circuit KOM: Final 10-mile loop with double-digit pitches rewarding the strong for top finishes.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant options from luxury resorts like Anantara Chiang Mai to budget guesthouses; book early for race weekend packages.
logistics.transport: GFNY Chiang Mai is accessible via Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX), with race HQ in central Chiang Mai. Expect police-escorted starts, fully supported aid stations, and finisher medals. Multiple distances available.

history.origin_story: GFNY Chiang Mai marks the series' expansion into northern Thailand, building on successes in Krabi and Khao Yai. Part of GFNY's aggressive Asia growth targeting 10 regional events.

final_verdict.one_liner: GFNY's tropical road epic: big climbs, Thai vibes, unbeatable value.

citations.label: GFNY announces second race in Thailand - GFNY World
